The Accountability
Paradox
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 24 --

How hard do you hold someone
accountable to a
project/task/deadline when there
are obvious outside factors
influencing the delivery?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 24 --

Example:
Your salesperson has a quota of selling 20 challenges this week
The salesperson comes to you on Wednesday and shows you that
due to black ice, there are XXX roads closed and nobody wants to
drive in to show up for their appointment. The salesperson then
proceeds to say they can close 20 in 9 business days, rather than 6.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 24 --

Do you hold her accountable for missing her
numbers or affirm her for re adjusting her
goal to be more realistic?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 24 --

If you prosecute her for re adjusting her
goal, you will probably discourage her from
confronting you in the future which could
decrease her performance
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 24 --

But if you don’t hold her accountable, then
people who are meeting their goals will feel
resentful and stupid.
Why did they work so hard to meet their
goals if this bozo missed her deadline???
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 24 --

But if you don’t hold her accountable, then
people who are meeting their goals will feel
resentful and stupid.
Why did they work so hard to meet their
goals if this bozo missed her deadline???
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 24 --

There are two assumptions
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 24 --

1.) Do you assume your employees are
creative, intelligent, and motivated?
OR
2.) Do you believe your employees are lazy,
conniving, and counting down the
minutes until they leave?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 24 --

If you believe they are lazy, you might as
well give up on them ever expressing any
creativity because you will not get it.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 24 --

It’s always better to assume that people
have good intentions unless they prove
you otherwise
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 24 --

Regardless, you still need to hold them
accountable so you don’t look like a dud.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 24 --

Let’s look at the 3 dynamics of
accountability in order to understand
what you must do...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 24 --

#1 Effort
Easy.
In order to become incredibly successful or
world class at anything you need
extraordinary effort. If someone is not
giving it, they need to be checked.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 24 --

#2 Promises
Most successful organizations have
external and internal promises. These
promises are regarding speed, value,
quality, and time of delivery.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 24 --

#2 Promises
Holding people to these promises is
CRUCIAL to getting things done. If you don’t
hold them to the promises, the promises
become meaningless, and therefore there
are no promises.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 24 --

#2 Promises
Some promises are easier to keep than
others. You need to decide which have too
many factors/parties involved to always be
upheld and which are hard promises.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 17 of 24 --

#2 Results
This is the hardest part of accountability.
Someone fails to deliver the result he/she
promised and is clearly responsible for...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 24 --

#2 Results
This is more complicated and therefore
needs to be filtered through 3 lenses
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 24 --

#2 Results
1.) Seniority/Track Record
2.) Degree of Difficulty
3.) Amount of Stupid
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 24 --

#2 Results
1.) Seniority/Track Record
You should expect more from someone who
has been at the company longer, as the
higher up they are the better decision
making and forecasting you should expect
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 24 --

#2 Results
1.) Degree of Difficulty
Some things are just HARD. EX: Hitting a
sales quota during a recession. When
deciding consequence of missing goal, take
into account difficulty beyond realm of
control.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 24 --

#2 Results
1.) Amount of Stupid
You don’t want to punish someone for taking
a risk but you don’t want to reward a stupid
risk. If someone missed the result, did they
take obviously stupid risks? Did they neglect
to consider consequences? Or were they
smart risks that just didn’t work out?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 23 of 24 --

Overall
It’s a balance between letting people take
risk and holding them too tightly
accountable.
Accountability is important but it’s not the
only thing to consider.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 24 of 24 --
